verses: sādhāraṇa⋅vandanā vande guru⋅pada⋅dvandvaṁ bhakta⋅vṛnda⋅samanvitam śrī⋅caitanya⋅prabhuṁ vande nityānanda⋅sahoditam śrī⋅nanda⋅nandanaṁ vande rādhikā⋅caraṇa⋅dvayam gopī⋅jana⋅samā⋅yuktaṁ vṛndāvana⋅manoharam śrī⋅bṛhad⋅gaṇoddeśa⋅dīpikā gurave gaura⋅candrāya rādhikāyai tadālaye kṛṣṇāya kṛṣṇa⋅bhaktāya tad⋅bhaktāya namo namaḥ śrī⋅guru⋅vandanā nāma⋅śreṣṭhaṁ manum api śacī⋅putram⋅atra svarūpaṁ rūpaṁ tasyāgrajam uru⋅purīṁ māthurīṁ goṣṭha⋅vāṭīm rādhā⋅kuṇḍaṁ girivaram⋅aho! rādhikā⋅mādhavāśāṁ prāpto jasya prathita⋅kṛpayā śrī⋅guruṁ taṁ nato’smi śrī⋅muktā⋅caritam namas te guru⋅devāya sarva⋅siddhi⋅pradāyine sarva⋅maṅgala⋅rūpāya sarvānanda⋅vidhāyine he śrī⋅guro jñānada dīna⋅bandho svānanda⋅dātaḥ karuṇaika⋅sindho vṛndāvanāsīna hitāvatāra prasīda rādhā⋅praṇaya⋅pracāra arcana paddhati trāyasva bho jagannātha guro saṁsāra⋅vahninā dagdhaṁ māṁ kāla⋅daṣṭaṁ ca tvām ahaṁ śaraṇaṁ gataḥ śrī⋅guru⋅rūpa⋅sakhī⋅vandanā rādhā⋅samukha⋅saṁśaktiṁ sakhī⋅saṅga⋅nivāsinīm tām⋅ahaṁ satataṁ vande mādhavāśraya⋅vigraham tvaṁ gopikā vṛṣaraves⋅tanayāntike’si, sevādhikāriṇi guro! nija⋅pāda⋅padme dāsyaṁ pradāya kuru māṁ vraja⋅kānane śrī⋅ rādhāṅghri⋅sevana⋅rase sukhinīṁ sukhābdhau śrī⋅vaiṣṇava⋅vandanā caitanya⋅candra⋅caritāmṛta⋅śuddha⋅sindhu⋅ vṛndāvanīya surasormi⋅samunnimagnāḥ je vai jagan⋅nija⋅guṇaiḥ svayam āpunanti tān vaiṣṇavāṁś⋅ca hari⋅nāma⋅parān namāmi caturyugīya vaiṣṇava⋅vandanā svayam⋅bhūr⋅nāradaḥ śambhuḥ kumāraḥ kapilo manuḥ prahalādo janako bhīṣmo balir vaiyāsakir vayam śrī.bhā. 6.3.20 mārkaṇḍeyo’ ambarīṣaś ca vasurvyāso vibhīṣaṇaḥ puṇḍarīko baliḥ śambhuḥ prahalādo viduro vaḥ dālbhyaḥ parāśaro bhīṣmo nāradādyāś ca vaiṣṇavaiḥ sevyā hariṁ niṣevyāmī no cedā⋅gaḥ paraṁ bhavet laghu⋅bhāgavatāmṛta uttara⋅khaṇḍe dbitīya saŋkhyādhṛta padmabākya śrīman⋅madhvācārya⋅vandanā ānanda⋅tīrtha⋅nāmā sukhamaya⋅dhāmā jatirjīyāt saṁsārārṇavataraṇīṁ jam⋅iha janāḥ kīrtayanti budhāḥ śrī⋅prameya⋅ratnāvalī śrīla⋅mādhavendra⋅purī⋅vandanā śrī⋅mādhavapurīṁ vande jatīndraṁ śiṣya⋅saṁyutam lokeṣvaṅkurito jena kṛṣṇa⋅bhakty⋅amarāṅghripaḥ śrī⋅vaiṣṇava⋅toṣanī śrīla⋅sanātana⋅gosvāmī⋅vandanā vairāgya⋅yug bhakti⋅rasaṁ prayatnair⋅ apāyayan⋅mām⋅anabhīpsum⋅andham kṛpām⋅budhir⋅yaḥ para⋅duḥkha⋅duḥkhī sanātanaṁ⋅taṁ prabhum⋅āśrayāmi śrī⋅vilāpa⋅kusumāñjaliḥ śrīla⋅rūpa⋅gosvāmī⋅vandanā ādadānas⋅tṛṇaṁ dantair⋅idaṁ jāce punaḥ punaḥ śrīmad⋅rūpa⋅padām⋅bhoja⋅dhūliḥ syāṁ janma⋅janmani śrī⋅muktā⋅caritam śrī⋅caitanyamano’bhīṣṭaṁ sthāpitaṁ jena bhūtale svayaṁ rūpaḥ kadā mahyaṁ dadāti sva⋅padāntikam śrī⋅prema⋅bhakti⋅candrikā śrīla⋅rūpa⋅raghunātha gosvāmī⋅vandanā rūpeti nāma vada bho rasane! sadā tvaṁ rūpañca saṁsmara manaḥ karuṇā⋅svarūpam rūpaṁ namaskaru śiraḥ sadayāvalokaṁ tasyā⋅dvitīya⋅sutanuṁ raghunātha⋅dāsam śrī⋅bhakti⋅ratnākara⋅dhṛta⋅sādhana⋅dīpikā śrīla⋅gopāla⋅bhaṭṭa⋅jīva⋅gosvāmī⋅vandanā sanātana⋅prema⋅pari⋅plutāntaraṁ śrī⋅rūpa⋅sakhyena vilakṣitākhilam namāmi rādhā⋅ramaṇaika⋅jīvanaṁ gopāla⋅bhaṭṭaṁ bhajatām⋅abhīṣṭa⋅dam śrī⋅rūpa⋅caraṇa⋅dvandva⋅rāgiṇaṁ vraja⋅vāsinam śrī⋅jīvaṁ satataṁ vande mandeṣv ānanda⋅dāyinam śrī⋅bhakti⋅ratnākara śrī⋅ṣaḍ⋅gosvāmī⋅vandanā śrī⋅rūpaṁ sāgrajaṁ vande raghunāthaṁ kṛpāmayam śrī⋅jīvaṁ bhaṭṭa⋅yugañca sajjana⋅sukha⋅dāyakam eṣāṁ sahaja⋅snigdhānāṁ pādareṇum⋅abhīkṣnaśaḥ sarva⋅vighna⋅vināśāya śirasā dhārayāmyaham śrī⋅pañca⋅tattva⋅vandanā pañca⋅tattvātmakaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhakta⋅rūpa⋅svarūpakam bhaktāvatāraṁ bhaktākhyaṁ namāmi bhakta⋅śaktikam vande gurūn īśa⋅bhaktānīśam⋅īśāvatārakān tat⋅prakāśāṁś⋅ca tac⋅chaktī kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅saṁjñakam śrī⋅caitanya⋅caritāmṛta hā viśvambhara! hā mahā⋅rasamaya! premika⋅sampannidhe! hā padmā⋅suta! hā dayārdra⋅hṛdaya! bhraṣṭaika⋅bandhūttama hā sīteśvara! hā carācara⋅pate! gaurāvatīrṇa⋅kṣama! hā śrīvāsa⋅gadādhareṣṭaviṣaya! tvaṁ me gatistvaṁ gatiḥ śrī⋅śrī⋅navadvīpa⋅śatakam śrī⋅śrīvāsa⋅vandanā śrīvāsa⋅paṇḍitaṁ naumi gaurāṅga⋅priya⋅pārṣadam jasya kṛpā⋅lavenāpi gaurāṅge jāyate ratiḥ śrīvāsa! kīrtanānanda! bhakta⋅goṣṭhy⋅eka⋅vallabha! tvāṁ namāmi mahā⋅yogin! bhakta⋅rūpo’si nāradaḥ praṇamāmi śrī⋅śrīvāsaṁ tamādyaṁ paṇḍitaṁ mudā śrī gaurāṅga⋅kṛpā⋅pātraṁ kīrtanānanda⋅mānasam śrī⋅gadādhara⋅paṇḍita⋅vandanā gadādhara⋅mahaṁ vande mādhvācārya⋅nandanam mahābhāva⋅svarūpaṁ śrī⋅caitanyābhinna⋅rūpiṇam gāndharvikā⋅svarūpāya gaurāṅga⋅prema⋅sampade gadādharāya me nityaṁ namo’stu he kṛpālave śrī⋅hlādinī⋅svarupaya gaurāṅga⋅suhṛdāya ca bhakta śakti⋅pradānāya gadādhara! namo’stu te śrī⋅śrī⋅advaita⋅prabhu⋅vandanā mahā⋅viṣṇur⋅jagat⋅kartā māyayā jaḥ sṛjaty⋅adaḥ tasyāvatāra evāyam⋅advaitācārya īśvaraḥ advaitaṁ hariṁ⋅ādvaitād⋅ācāryaṁ bhakti⋅śaṁsanāt bhaktāvatāram⋅īśaṁ tam⋅advaitācāryam⋅āśraye śrī⋅svarūpa⋅gosvāmī⋅kaḍacā śrī⋅advaita! namas⋅tubhyaṁ kali⋅jana⋅kṛpā⋅nidhe gaura⋅prema⋅pradānāya śrī⋅sītāpataye namaḥ prema⋅bhakti⋅pradaṁ śrīman⋅mādhavendra⋅purī⋅priyam śrīlādvaita⋅prabhuṁ vande śrī⋅mādhava⋅sampradāyinam śrī⋅bhakti⋅ratnākara advaitāya namaste’stu maheśāya mahātmane jasya prasādāc caitanya caraṇe jāyate ratiḥ śrī⋅śrī⋅nityānanda⋅prabhu⋅vandanā nityānanda namas⋅tubhyaṁ premānanda⋅pradāyine kalau kalmaṣa⋅nāśāya jāhnavāpataye namaḥ hāḍāi⋅paṇḍita⋅tanuja! kṛpā⋅samudra! padmāvatī⋅tanaya! tīrtha⋅padāravinda! tvaṁ prema⋅kalpatarur⋅ārti⋅harāvatāra! māṁ pāhi pāmaram⋅anātha⋅mananya⋅bandhum nirānandam idaṁ sarvaṁ premānandāspadī⋅kṛtaṁ jena taṁ satataṁ vande nityānandaṁ jagadgurum nityānandam ahaṁ vande karṇe lasita⋅mauktikam caitanyāgraja⋅rūpeṇa pāvitrīkṛta⋅bhūtalam audāryeṇa sukāmadhenu⋅diviṣadvṛkṣendu⋅cintāmaṇi⋅ vṛndaṁ brahma⋅sukhañca sundaratayā kandarpa⋅vṛndaṁ prabhum vātsalyena sumātṛ⋅dhenu⋅nicayaṁ vispardhanaṁ nityānandam ahaṁ namāmi satataṁ premābdhi⋅saṁvardhanam śrīman⋅nityānanda⋅candraṁ karuṇāmaya⋅vigraham caitanyābhinna⋅dehaṁ taṁ vande sarvajana⋅priyam śrī⋅śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅mahāprabhu⋅vandanā dhyeyaṁ sadā paribhava⋅ghnamabhīṣṭa⋅doham tīrthāspadaṁ śiva⋅viriñci⋅nutaṁ śaraṇyam bhṛtyārti⋅haṁ praṇata⋅pāla bhavābdhi⋅potam vande mahā⋅puruṣa te caraṇāravindam tyaktvā sudustyaja⋅surepsita⋅rājya⋅lakṣmīm dharmiṣṭha ārya⋅vacasā jadagā daraṇyam māyā⋅mṛgaṁ dayitayepsitam anvadhāvad vande mahā⋅puruṣa te caraṇāravindam śrī.bhā. 11.5.33-34 ānanda⋅līlā⋅maya⋅vigrahāya hemābha⋅divyac⋅chavi⋅sundarāya tasmai mahā⋅prema⋅rasa⋅pradāya caitanya⋅candrāya namo namaste śrī⋅caitanya⋅candrāmṛtama jaya navadvīpa⋅nava⋅pradīpa⋅ prabhāvaḥ pāṣaṇḍa⋅gajaika⋅siŋhaḥ sva⋅nāma⋅saŋkhyā⋅japa⋅sūtra⋅dhārī, caitanya⋅candro bhagavān⋅murāriḥ sa jayati viśuddha⋅vikramaḥ kanakābhaḥ kamalāyatekṣaṇaḥ vara⋅jānu⋅vilambi⋅ṣaḍ⋅bhujo bahudhā bhakti⋅rasābhinartakaḥ śrī⋅caitanya⋅bhāgavata saṁsāra⋅duḥkha⋅jaladhau patitasya kāma⋅ krodhādi⋅nakra⋅makaraiḥ kavalī⋅kṛtasya durvāsanā⋅nigaḍitasya nirāśrayasya caitanya⋅candra mama dehi padāvalambam śrī⋅caitanya⋅candrāmṛtama caitanya⋅candra mama hṛt⋅kumudaṁ vikāśya hṛdyaṁ vidhehi nija⋅cintana⋅bhṛṅga⋅raṅgaiḥ kiñcāparādha⋅timiraṁ nibiḍaṁ vidhūya pādāmṛtaṁ sadaya pāyaya durgataṁ mām śrī⋅stavāvali anarpita⋅carīṁ cirāt karuṇayāvatīrṇaḥ kalau samarpayitum⋅unnatojvala⋅rasāṁ sva⋅bhakti⋅śriyam hariḥ puraṭa⋅sundara⋅dyuti⋅kadamba⋅sandīpitaḥ sadā hṛdaya⋅kandare sphuratu vaḥ śacī⋅nandanaḥ śrī⋅vidagdha⋅mādhavaḥ rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅praṇaya⋅vikṛti⋅halādinī⋅śaktir⋅asmad ekātmānāv⋅api bhuvi purā deha⋅bhedaṁ gatau⋅tau caitanyākhyaṁ prakaṭam⋅adhunā tad⋅dvayañ⋅caikyam⋅āptaṁ rādhā⋅bhāva⋅dyuti⋅suvalitaṁ naumi kṛṣṇa⋅svarūpam śrī⋅svarūpa⋅gosvāmī⋅kaḍacā śrī⋅śrī⋅gaura⋅nityānanda⋅vandanā ājānu⋅lambita⋅bhujau kanakāvadātau saṅkīrtanaika⋅pitarau kamalāyatākṣau viśvambharau dvijavarau juga⋅dharma⋅ pālau vande jagat⋅priya⋅karau karuṇāvatārau śrī⋅caitanya⋅bhāgavata vande śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅nityānandau sahoditau gauḍodaye puṣpavantau citrau śandau tamo⋅nudau śrī⋅caitanya⋅caritāmṛta avatīrṇau sa⋅kāruṇau paricchinnau sad⋅īśvarau śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅caitanya⋅nityānandau dvau bhrātarau bhaje śrī⋅murāri⋅gupta⋅kṛta śloka śrī⋅navadvīpa⋅dhāma⋅vandanā śrutiś⋅chāndogyākhyā vadati paramaṁ brahmapurakaṁ smṛti⋅vaikuṇṭhākhyaṁ vadati kila jad⋅viṣṇu⋅sadanam sita⋅dvīpañcānya virala⋅rasiko’yaṁ vraja⋅vanaṁ navadvīpaṁ vande parama⋅sukhadaṁ taṁ cid⋅uditam śrī⋅navadvīpa⋅śatakam navīna⋅śrī⋅bhaktiṁ nava⋅kanaka⋅gaurākṛti⋅patiṁ navāraṇya⋅śreṇī⋅nava⋅sura⋅sarid⋅vāta⋅valitam navīna⋅śrī⋅rādhā⋅hari⋅rasamayotkīrtana⋅vidhiṁ navadvīpaṁ vande nava⋅karuṇa⋅mādyan nava⋅rucim śrī⋅navadvīpa⋅śataka
en_translation: sādhāraṇa-vandanā (General Obeisance) I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of the Spiritual Master and the assembly of devotees. I offer my respectful obeisances to Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, who has risen along with Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu. I offer my respectful obeisances to Śrī Nanda⋅nandana and the lotus feet of Śrīmati Rādhikā, accompanied by the gopīs, who make Vṛndāvana so enchanting. I offer my respectful obeisances to the Spiritual Master, to Lord Gaura⋅candra, to Śrīmati Rādhikā and Her abode, to Lord Kṛṣṇa, to His devotees, and to the devotees of His devotees. śrī-guru-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Guru) I bow down to that Śrī Guru, by whose abundant mercy I have attained the greatest name, the mantra, Śrī Śacī⋅nandana here, Śrī Svarūpa Dāmodara, Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī and his elder brother Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, the great city of Mathurā, the cowherd village, Śrī Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Govardhana Hill, and the hope of attaining Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava. Obeisance to you, O Gurudeva, the bestower of all perfection, the embodiment of all auspiciousness, and the source of all bliss. O Śrī Guru, giver of divine knowledge, friend of the destitute, bestower of your own bliss, ocean of mercy, residing in Vṛndāvana! O incarnation of welfare! Please be merciful to me, O preacher of Rādhā's love. Save me, O Master of the Universe, O Guru! I am burnt by the fire of material existence and bitten by the serpent of time. I have taken shelter of you. śrī-guru-rūpa-sakhī-vandanā (Obeisance to Guru as a Sakhī) I constantly offer obeisances to her, who is the abode of shelter for Mādhava, the cohesive energy facing Rādhā, residing in the company of the sakhīs. You are a gopī near the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu. O Guru, having the authority of service! Having given me the service of your own lotus feet, please make me happy in the ocean of bliss, in the mellow of serving the feet of Śrī Rādhā in the forest of Vraja. śrī-vaiṣṇava-vandanā (Obeisance to Vaiṣṇavas) I offer obeisances to those Vaiṣṇavas who are fully submerged in the waves of the excellent nectar of Vṛndāvana, which is the pure ocean of the nectar of Śrī Caitanyacandra's character, and who purify the world by their own qualities and are devoted to the Holy Name of Hari. caturyugīya vaiṣṇava-vandanā (Obeisance to Vaiṣṇavas of the Four Ages) Svayambhū, Nārada, Śambhu, the Kumāras, Kapila, Manu, Prahlāda, Janaka, Bhīṣma, Bali, Vaiyāsaki, and us. Mārkaṇḍeya, Ambarīṣa, Vasu, Vyāsa, Vibhīṣaṇa, Puṇḍarīka, Bali, Śambhu, Prahlāda, Vidura, and Dhruva. Dālbhya, Parāśara, Bhīṣma, Nārada, and others are to be served by the Vaiṣṇavas. I serve Hari; otherwise, I would commit a great offense. śrīman-madhvācārya-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Madhvācārya) May the renunciate named Ānanda Tīrtha be victorious! He is an abode of happiness, and the wise praise him as the boat for crossing the ocean of material existence. śrīla-mādhavendra-purī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī) I offer obeisances to Śrīla Mādhavendra Purī, the best of sannyāsīs, along with his disciples. By him, the wish-fulfilling tree of Kṛṣṇa-bhakti was made to sprout in this world. śrīla-sanātana-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī) I take shelter of that master, Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, the ocean of mercy, who is distressed by the suffering of others. With great effort, he made me drink the nectar of devotion mixed with renunciation, even though I was unwilling and blind. śrīla-rūpa-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī) Holding a straw between my teeth, I beg this again and again: may I become the dust of the lotus feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī birth after birth. When will Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī himself give me the shelter of his own feet? He who established on this earth the innermost heart's desire of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu. śrīla-rūpa-raghunātha gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Rūpa and Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmīs) O tongue! Always chant the name "Rūpa". O mind! Remember Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, the embodiment of mercy. Bow your head to Śrīla Rūpa, whose glance is full of compassion, and to his second beautiful form, Śrīla Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. śrīla-gopāla-bhaṭṭa-jīva-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrīla Gopāla Bhaṭṭa and Jīva Gosvāmīs) I bow to Śrīla Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī, who fulfills the desires of the devotees, whose heart is flooded with eternal love, who is distinguished by the friendship of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, and whose only life is Śrī Rādhā-Ramaṇa. I constantly offer obeisances to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, who is attached to the lotus feet of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, who is a resident of Vraja, and who gives bliss to the ignorant. śrī-ṣaḍ-gosvāmī-vandanā (Obeisance to the Six Gosvāmīs) I offer obeisances to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī along with his elder brother, to the merciful Raghunātha Dāsa, to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, and to the two Bhaṭṭas, who bestow happiness upon the saintly. I hold on my head the dust of the feet of these Gosvāmīs, who are naturally affectionate, again and again, for the destruction of all obstacles. śrī-pañca-tattva-vandanā (Obeisance to the Pañca-tattva) I bow to Lord Kṛṣṇa, who appears as the Pañca-tattva: in the form of a devotee, the manifestation of a devotee, the incarnation of a devotee, the pure devotee, and the devotional energy. I offer obeisances to the Gurus, the devotees of the Lord, the Lord Himself, His incarnations, His manifestations, and His energies. I bow to the Lord known as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya. O Viśvambhara! O abode of the great nectar of mellows! O ocean of love! O son of mother Śacī! O You whose heart melts with compassion! You are the best friend of the fallen! O Lord of Sītā! O Master of the moving and non-moving! O You who are capable of bringing down the Gaura avatar! O object of affection for Śrīvāsa and Gadādhara! You are my shelter, You are my goal. śrī-śrīvāsa-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita) I praise Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, the dear associate of Lord Gaurāṅga. By even a drop of his mercy, attachment to Lord Gaurāṅga is born. O Śrīvāsa! O bliss of kīrtana! O beloved of the assembly of devotees! I bow to you, O great mystic! You are Nārada Muni in the form of a devotee. I bow with joy to that Śrīvāsa, the foremost Paṇḍita, the recipient of Śrī Gaurāṅga's mercy, whose mind finds bliss in kīrtana. śrī-gadādhara-paṇḍita-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gadādhara Paṇḍita) I offer obeisances to Śrī Gadādhara Paṇḍita, the delight of Śrī Madhvācārya, the embodiment of Mahābhāva, whose form is non-different from Śrī Caitanya. Obeisances forever to Śrī Gadādhara, who is the form of Śrīmati Rādhā⋅rāṇī, the wealth of Lord Gaurāṅga's love. O merciful one! O Gadādhara! Obeisances to you, the embodiment of the Hlādinī-śakti, the companion of Lord Gaurāṅga, the bestower of devotional energy. śrī-śrī-advaita-prabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Advaita Prabhu) Mahā-Viṣṇu, the creator of the universe, who creates this world through Māyā; this Advaita Ācārya is indeed His avatar, the Supreme Controller. I take shelter of that Advaita Ācārya, who is the Supreme Lord, the incarnation of a devotee. He is called 'Advaita' because He is non-different from Hari, and 'Ācārya' because He teaches devotion. O Śrī Advaita! Obeisances to you, the ocean of mercy for the people of Kali-yuga. Obeisances to the Lord of Śrī Sītā Ṭhākurāṇī, the bestower of love for Gaura. I offer obeisances to Śrīla Advaita Prabhu, the giver of loving devotion, who is dear to Śrīman Mādhavendra Purī and belongs to the Śrī Mādhava Sampradāya. Obeisances to Advaita, the Great Lord, the Great Soul, by whose mercy attachment to the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya is born. śrī-śrī-nityānanda-prabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu) O Nityānanda! Obeisances to You, the giver of the bliss of divine love. Obeisances to the Lord of Jāhnavā Devī, for the destruction of the impurities of Kali-yuga. O son of Hāḍāi Paṇḍita! O ocean of mercy! O son of Padmāvatī! Your lotus feet are the shelter of all holy places! You are the wish-fulfilling tree of love, the avatar who removes all distress! Please protect me, a sinner, an orphan who has no other friend. I constantly offer obeisances to that Nityānanda, the Spiritual Master of the Universe, by whom this entire joyless world was made the abode of the bliss of love. I offer obeisances to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu, who has a pearl shining in His ear, and who purified the earth by appearing as the elder brother of Śrī Caitanya. I constantly bow to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu, the Lord who increases the ocean of love. In generosity, He surpasses the wish-fulfilling cows, celestial trees, moons, and gems. In beauty, He defeats the Cupids. He is the reservoir of the happiness of Brahman. In motherly affection, He challenges the herds of cows. I offer obeisances to Śrīman Nityānanda Candra, the embodiment of mercy, whose body is non-different from Śrī Caitanya, and who is dear to everyone. śrī-śrī-gaurāṅga-mahāprabhu-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gaurāṅga Mahāprabhu) I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet, O Great Personality! You are always to be meditated upon. You destroy the insult of material life and fulfill all desires. You are the abode of all holy places, praised by Śiva and Brahmā. You are the only shelter, the reliever of the distress of Your servants, the protector of the surrendered, and the boat for crossing the ocean of material existence. I offer obeisances to Your lotus feet, O Great Personality! Abandoning the opulence of royalty desired by the gods, which is hard to renounce, You, the most righteous one, went to the forest in honor of the word of a brāhmaṇa, and ran after the illusionary deer desired by Your beloved. Obeisances, obeisances to You, O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Your form is full of blissful pastimes. You are beautiful with a brilliant golden luster, and You are the bestower of the great nectar of love. All glories to the power of the new lamp of Navadvīpa, the lion who defeats the elephants of atheism! Śrī Caitanyacandra, the Supreme Lord Murāri, holds the chanting beads of His own Holy Name. He is victorious! His valor is pure, He shines like gold, His eyes are large like lotus petals, His arms extend to His excellent knees, and He dances, manifesting the nectar of devotion in many ways. O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Please give me the support of Your lotus feet. I have fallen into the ocean of the misery of material existence, swallowed by the crocodiles and sharks of lust, anger, and greed, fettered by wicked desires, and I am without shelter. O Śrī Caitanyacandra! Make the lily of my heart bloom and delight it with the play of the bumblebee of Your own remembrance. Having shaken off the dense darkness of offenses, O merciful one, please make me, this unfortunate soul, drink the nectar of Your lotus feet. May that son of Mother Śacī always shine in the cave of your heart. He is Śrī Hari Himself, glowing with a multitude of beautiful golden rays. He descended in the Age of Kali out of mercy to bestow the wealth of His own devotion, the elevated brilliant mellow of Vraja, which had not been offered for a long time. I praise the form of Kṛṣṇa, now appearing as Śrī Caitanya, with the luster of the emotions of Śrī Rādhā. These two—the Hlādinī-śakti and the Śaktimān—are eternally one identity, yet previously on earth, They attained separate bodies. Now, They have again obtained unity in the form of Śrī Caitanya. śrī-śrī-gaura-nityānanda-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Gaura and Nityānanda) I offer obeisances to the two compassionate avatars, Śrī Gaura and Śrī Nityānanda, who make the world happy. They are the protectors of the religion of the age. Their arms extend to Their knees, Their complexion is golden, They are the fathers of the Saṅkīrtana movement, Their eyes are broad like lotuses. They are the sustainers of the universe and the best of the brāhmaṇas. I offer obeisances to Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Śrī Nityānanda, who have risen together on the horizon of Gauḍa like the sun and the moon. They are wonderful, bestowers of all auspiciousness, and dispellers of the darkness of ignorance. I worship the two brothers, Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Śrī Nityānanda, who have descended to this world. They are the embodiments of compassion, accompanied by Their associates, and are the Masters of the saintly. śrī-navadvīpa-dhāma-vandanā (Obeisance to Śrī Navadvīpa Dhāma) I offer obeisances to that Navadvīpa, which gives supreme happiness. It is the spiritually risen forest of Vraja. The Chāndogya Upaniṣad calls it the city of the Supreme Brahman, the Smṛti scriptures call it the abode of Viṣṇu named Vaikuṇṭha, others call it Śvetadvīpa, and a rare Rasika calls it the forest of Vraja. I offer obeisances to Śrī Navadvīpa, which maddens everyone with new mercy and possesses a new taste. It is the abode of the Master of the new golden form, Lord Gaura, and the new devotion. It is surrounded by rows of new forests and refreshed by breezes from the new celestial Gaṅgā. It is the place for the performance of the new loud chanting, full of the nectar of Śrī Śrī Rādhā and Hari.
